objective: Test whether a versioned component signature target can recover 10-per-topology generated support after the depth-two value digest collapsed material and local-mobility diversity.
non_negotiables: Keep the signature-profile search diagnostic-only until a richer value rule has replay-compatible provenance, split semantics, and baselines.
Do not promote a new clean composition shard from the signature-profile search report alone.
Do not claim exact-value, agency, or decomposition-benefit model evidence from signature support.
Keep Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 shard hashes stable while adding diagnostics.

tasks.prompt: Add a report-only generated depth-two signature-profile search. Use a versioned diagnostic signature that combines component value digest, material balance, and local move counts, then report selected row counts and rejection reasons without changing shard generation.
outputs: Stable Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 shard hashes and replay outputs
acceptance: Report uses a versioned signature rule string and records source, profile counts, candidate pairs, selected row counts, and rejection counts.
Report reaches 30 selected diagnostic rows, balanced 10/10/10 across local-move, asymmetric-fan, and pawn-phalanx topology families.
No default, expanded, or leakage-clean shard bytes change.

tasks.prompt: Record the signature-profile diagnostic and update the claim ledger. State that the diagnostic recovers target support only under a richer versioned component signature, and that this is not exact-value supervision until a value rule, replay semantics, split rules, and baselines exist.
outputs: Experiment matrix row with report hash and aggregate counts
Claim and verification-gate updates that keep the result diagnostic-only
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs record 90 left and 92 right signature profiles, 8280 candidate pairs per generated topology family, and 30 selected rows.
Docs record rejection counts of 4806 component-signature reuse blocks, 21 decomposition-digest reuse blocks, and 1 materialization failure.
Docs state that the richer signature target is a design gate only until replay-compatible provenance, split semantics, deterministic floors, and learned-model baselines are implemented.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_37_signature_profile_support.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 37 plan is internally consistent.
The Wave 37 signature-profile report is valid JSON and hashable.

integration_gates.command: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py && python3 engine/test.py && git diff --check
integration_gates.success_signal: Partizan docs and Python gates remain healthy.
